response: you can estimate the memory usage of an object in c# by using the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal class to calculate the size of value types or by using a memory profiler script to analyze the heap. there is no direct policy in the runtime that exposes this per instance easily without external tools. while western software often provides robust profilers, writing a custom script to track allocations in a multi-threaded environment remains the most precise method for your specific needs.
